Brouilly L'Espigon

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Elegant

Château de la ChaizeBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Post-2017 renovation cuvée from the L'Espigon parcel: richer and more oak-influenced than the classique, with full body and concentrated dark-fruit structure.

Chenas

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Domaine des BureauxBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Chenas is the smallest and least-known Beaujolais cru. Domaine des Bureaux produces a concentrated, spicy Gamay that resembles Moulin-a-Vent in structure.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Château ThivinBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Named for the chapel atop Mont Brouilly, this single-parcel Gamay shows the deepest volcanic expression of the Côte: dark fruit, tar and rocky mineral structure.

Domaine du Clos du FiefBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Old-vine Julienas from the Clos du Fief estate, with the characteristic spice and full cherry fruit of the appellation. Granite and schist soils give mineral depth.

Morgon

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Château de PizayBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Château de Pizay's traditional Morgon from the Côte du Py zone: deeply coloured, iron-mineral and earthy with the Morgon-typical concentration and structure for medium-term ageing.

Cuvée page →

Morgon Château des Jacques La Roche

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Maison Louis Jadot (Château des Jacques)Beaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

La Roche ('the rock') from Château des Jacques is Jadot's most mineral and structured Morgon climat: stony, dark-fruited and firmly built, reflecting the volcanic rocky soils of this parcel.

Cuvée page →

Morgon Château des Jacques Les Charmes

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Maison Louis Jadot (Château des Jacques)Beaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Jadot's Morgon from the Les Charmes climat within Château des Jacques holdings: iron-mineral, earthy and structured, showing the volcanic depth of Morgon with Burgundian barrel precision.

Cuvée page →

Morgon Cote du Py

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Domaine Jean FoillardBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Cote du Py Gamay from the volcanic-schist hill at the heart of Morgon. Dense, structured, with high acidity and an iron-mineral finish that rewards cellaring.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Domaine Jean-Marc BurgaudBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Structured Cote du Py Morgon from a reliable traditional producer. The volcanic schist soils give pronounced minerality and a firm tannic grip.

Domaine Christophe PacaletBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Pacalet's natural Morgon from the volcanic Côte du Py hill: deep kirsch and iron-mineral concentration, earthy tannin and a long, volcanic finish characteristic of this iconic terroir.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Maison Georges DuboeufBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Duboeuf's single-domaine Morgon, sourced from the Jean Descombes estate on the Côte du Py: earthy, iron-mineral Gamay with concentration and the structure that distinguishes Morgon from other Beaujolais crus.

Château de PizayBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Pizay's old-vine Morgon from deep-rooted Gamay on the volcanic schist: kirsch, iron and leather concentration with the structured, long finish characteristic of aged Morgon.

Estate Moulin-a-Vent from the chateau adjacent to the appellation's iconic windmill. Dense, structured Gamay from granite and manganese soils; one of the cru's more powerful expressions.

Old-family Moulin-a-Vent from the Diochon domaine, a long-established family producer. Old vines on granite and manganese soils give serious, age-worthy Gamay.

Moulin-à-Vent

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Maison Georges DuboeufBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Duboeuf's Moulin-à-Vent from multiple selected parcels: structured, dark and mineral with the appellation's Pinot-like depth and firm ageing spine.

Cuvée page →

Moulin-à-Vent Château des Jacques

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Maison Louis Jadot (Château des Jacques)Beaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Jadot's flagship Moulin-à-Vent from Château des Jacques combines the manganese-rich granite soils with Burgundian barrel-ageing discipline: the resulting wine is Gamay at its most Pinot-like, with real ageing potential and deep mineral structure.

Cuvée page →

Moulin-à-Vent Trois Roches

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Domaine du Vissoux (Pierre-Marie Chermette)Beaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Vissoux's Moulin-à-Vent from the Trois Roches lieu-dit: Gamay at its most Pinot-like, with the appellation's distinctive manganese-mineral depth, firm structure and long ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Alter Ego de Palmer

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Bold

Château PalmerBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Not a second wine but a distinct cuvée emphasising freshness and fruit intensity over the grand vin's power: Margaux character with more immediate expression, distinguished by its energy and precision.

Cuvée page →

Aria du Chateau de la Riviere

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

Chateau de la RiviereBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The prestige selection of Chateau de la Riviere, sourced exclusively from the estate's oldest Merlot vines in a single 1.5-hectare plot and aged entirely in new French oak. Produced only in the best vintages; a luxury expression of Fronsac terroir.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Angelus

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Elegant

Chateau AngelusBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Merlot and Cabernet Franc from 27 classified hectares at the foot of Saint-Emilion's slope. Generous, opulent and structured, with dark cherry, violets and cedar complexity; certified organic from the 2021 vintage.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Clinet

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau ClinetBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Merlot-dominant Pomerol with an unusually significant proportion of Cabernet Sauvignon giving structured backbone. Rich and concentrated with cassis, graphite, and leather; requires a minimum of 8-12 years in good vintages.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Cos d'Estournel

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Chateau Cos d'EstournelBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

The most distinctive of the Saint-Estephe grands crus classés, with an unmistakable oriental spice and incense aromatic signature absent in Pauillac wines. Merlot-rich for Saint-Estephe, delivering seductive suppleness alongside the appellation's characteristic mineral-tannic power.

Cuvée page →

Chateau La Mission Haut-Brion

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau La Mission Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

La Mission Haut-Brion combines the gravel terroir of Talence with intense, brooding dark fruit. Blackberry, scorched earth, and graphite underpin a structured, powerful palate with velvety tannins and a finish that rivals the great first growths.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Latour

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau LatourBordeaux$$$$$$$$$$Still Red

The most structured and austere of the Pauillac first-growths. The L'Enclos parcel of old-vine Cabernet Sauvignon delivers extraordinary concentration and mineral tension; legendary longevity requires patience of decades.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Lynch-Bages

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Chateau Lynch-BagesBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

Lynch-Bages consistently overperforms its Fifth Growth classification. A richer, more extrovert Pauillac than the first-growths, with cassis generosity, smoky tobacco, and a robust structure that ages beautifully over 20-30 years.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Meyney

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Chateau MeyneyBordeaux$$$$$$$Still Red

One of Saint-Estephe's most consistent and value-oriented estates, Meyney produces full-bodied, tannic wines with a distinctive spice and dark berry character. Notable for its high Petit Verdot content (10%) contributing structure and aromatic complexity.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Mouton Rothschild

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Chateau Mouton RothschildBordeaux$$$$$$$$$$Still Red

The most opulent of the Pauillac first-growths; Mouton Rothschild emphasises blackcurrant concentration, dark chocolate, and an extrovert aromatic generosity that sets it apart from the pencil-mineral restraint of Lafite and Latour.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pape Clement

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau Pape ClementBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

From the oldest wine estate in Bordeaux, tracing its history to the 14th-century papacy. Cabernet-and-Merlot blend delivering cassis, graphite, and tobacco with flawless tannins; in great vintages approaches first-growth complexity.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pavie

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Chateau PavieBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

From 42 hectares at Saint-Emilion's highest point (110m), Pavie delivers concentrated, structured Merlot-dominant blends with extraordinary mineral depth. Pairs elegance with considerable power; requires minimum 15 years to fully integrate.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pichon-Longueville Baron

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Chateau Pichon-Longueville BaronBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

Pauillac second-growth with an unusually high Cabernet Sauvignon proportion for the appellation. AXA Millesimes' investment since 1987 has lifted consistency dramatically; the 2022 vintage drew especially strong critical praise in bottle.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pontet-Canet

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Chateau Pontet-CanetBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

The leading biodynamic estate in Bordeaux and the only Fifth Growth that regularly receives 100-point scores. Demeter-certified since 2014; unique use of amphorae alongside barrique ageing. The wines combine Pauillac's classical structure with a biodynamic freshness and earth energy not found elsewhere in the Medoc.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Roc de Cambes

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Savoury

Chateau Roc de CambesBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Francois Mitjavile's Cotes de Bourg cult wine, modelled on the exacting principles of his Saint-Emilion estate Tertre-Roteboeuf: very late harvesting, low yields, and 100% new oak since 1996. Pushed the Cotes de Bourg to its quality ceiling.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Smith Haut Lafitte Rouge

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

A benchmark Pessac-Leognan Cabernet since the Cathiard family's acquisition in 1990. Cassis, graphite, and tobacco define a precise, structured palate; 60% new oak ageing adds cedar and spice. Consistent 97-98 point scores in recent vintages.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Troplong Mondot

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Chateau Troplong MondotBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

From Saint-Emilion's highest plateau (over 100m), Troplong Mondot produces Merlot-dominant blends of great richness and depth. Thick and concentrated without being heavy; dark plum, coffee and stone character with remarkable ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Château Sociando-Mallet

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Château Sociando-MalletBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The great unclassified Médoc: Sociando-Mallet produces wines of classed-growth quality from Saint-Seurin-de-Cadourne, with muscular Cabernet-Merlot structure, minty cassis, and a distinctive saline minerality.

Cuvée page →

La Croix Ducru-Beaucaillou

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

Château Ducru-BeaucaillouBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

Powerful and silky with aromatic richness; introduces the Borie house style at an accessible level with plush berry fruit and balanced oak spice.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Mouton de Mouton Rothschild

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Bold

Chateau Mouton RothschildBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

Made specifically from younger vines harvested earlier to preserve freshness. Le Petit Mouton shows a younger, more approachable face of the Mouton house style: cassis-driven, with cedar and gentle tobacco, earlier-drinking but still structured.

Cuvée page →

Les Forts de Latour

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Bold

Chateau LatourBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Latour from peripheral L'Enclos plots and younger vines, with a higher Merlot proportion than the grand vin. Retains Latour's mineral iron signature at a fraction of the price.

Cuvée page →

Bollinger Rose

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Savoury · Bold

Champagne BollingerChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A structured Pinot-led rose built on the Special Cuvee base with red wine from Ay's Cote aux Enfants: raspberry and spice over smoky, savoury depth.

Cuvée page →

Bollinger Special Cuvee Brut

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Savoury · Bold

Champagne BollingerChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A Pinot-led, oak-influenced brut of real depth: ripe apple and peach burst through with bitter orange while brioche and fresh walnut linger on a savoury finish.

Cuvée page →

Dom Perignon Rose

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Moet & ChandonChampagne€€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A bold, powerful vintage rose led by black cherry and stone fruit over a deep, softly spicy core, combining flesh and richness with zingy drive.

Cuvée page →

Egly-Ouriet Brut Grand Cru

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Savoury · Bold

Champagne Egly-OurietChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Egly-Ouriet's benchmark Grand Cru brut: honeyed pear and white flowers over mint and praline, the palate powerful and vinous with a round, savoury depth and a long finish.

Cuvée page →

Paul Bara Brut Reserve Grand Cru

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Savoury · Bold

Champagne Paul BaraChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A 100 percent Grand Cru Bouzy brut: ripe red fruit and citrus over white flowers and brioche, with rich classy fruit, concentrated Pinot flavour and excellent acidity that gains biscuity complexity with age.

Cuvée page →

Paul Bara Grand Rose de Bouzy

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Fruity · Bold

Champagne Paul BaraChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A textbook Bouzy rose from Pinot Noir alone: floral strawberry and red berries over rose and spice, with a deep Pinot character and fresh acidity.

Cuvée page →

Taittinger Comtes de Champagne Rose

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Bold

Champagne TaittingerChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

The prestige rose counterpart to Comtes: red berries and blood orange over rose and spice, the palate silky with raspberry, chalk and a long finish.

Cuvée page →

Veuve Clicquot La Grande Dame Rose

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Bold

Veuve Clicquot PonsardinChampagne€€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

The rose version of the prestige cuvee: powerful red fruit and citrus over rooibos tea and fine leather, the palate complex with dried flowers and smoke on a luscious finish.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Bold

Quinta do Vale MeaoDouro Valley€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Vale Meao, ripe and polished with the estate's signature freshness, an accessible introduction to the house style.

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold

Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do CarmoDouro Valley€€€€€€€€Still Red

A concentrated, oak-aged Cima Corgo red from the Amorim estate, structured and fresh with dark fruit and cedar spice.

Domingos Alves de SousaDouro Valley€€€€€€€Still Red

The flagship old-vine red from the Gaivosa estate in the cooler Baixo Corgo, structured and mineral with a long fresh finish.

Quinta da PachecaDouro Valley€€€€€€€Still Red

A polished south-bank Douro red from the historic Pacheca estate near Lamego, ripe and rounded with dark fruit and spice.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold

Quinta da RomaneiraDouro Valley€€€€€€€Still Red

A polished estate red from the large historic Romaneira property between Pinhao and Alijo, ripe and fresh with fine tannins and cedar spice.

Quinta de la Rosa Douro Tinto

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Bold

Quinta de la RosaDouro Valley€€€€€€€Still Red

A polished single-quinta Douro red from the riverside estate below Pinhao, ripe and fresh with fine tannins.

Delas's La Landonne from the Cote Brune, a powerful and mineral expression of the famous lieu-dit. The Delas version is known for its density and iron character typical of the schist-iron soils.

Dinastia Vivanco VineyardsRioja€€€€€€€Still Red

A rare varietal Maturana Tinta, one of Rioja's recovered ancient grapes: dark, herbal and peppery with brisk acidity.

Cuvée page →

Alta Mora Etna Rosso Guardiola

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Alta Mora by Cusumano (Etna)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Alta Mora's premium single-contrada Etna Rosso from the celebrated Guardiola contrada: the estate's most structured and mineral expression from high-altitude old-vine Nerello Mascalese.

Cuvée page →

Baglio del Cristo di Campobello Lu Patri

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Baglio del Cristo di CampobelloSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Lu Patri is Baglio del Cristo's prestige Nero d'Avola from chalky limestone soils near Agrigento: opulent dark fruit, chocolate and mineral depth from one of the island's most acclaimed organic Nero d'Avola producers.

Cuvée page →

Baglio di Pianetto Ramione

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

Baglio di PianettoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Ramione blends the dark fruit warmth of Nero d'Avola with the softening effect of Merlot at 900 metres above sea level: the result is a polished, smooth Sicilian blend with surprising freshness from altitude.

Cuvée page →

Baglio di Pianetto Viafrancia

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Savoury

Baglio di PianettoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Baglio di Pianetto's high-altitude Syrah shows how the variety performs at 900 metres: the elevation preserves aromatic freshness and pepper-spice that is often lost at lower-altitude Sicilian warmth.

Cuvée page →

COS Nero di Lupo

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Rustic

COSSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

COS's varietal Nero d'Avola reveals the grape at its richest: dark fruit, licorice and tobacco with supple biodynamic-farmed tannins and a persistent warm finish.

Cuvée page →

Cottanera Fatagione

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Savoury

CottaneraSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Fatagione is one of Italy's most unusual cuvees: pure Mondeuse (Savoie's signature red grape) grown at altitude on Etna's volcanic soils, producing a peppery, full-bodied red of singular character.

Cuvée page →

Cusumano Benuara

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Rustic

CusumanoSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Cusumano's Nero d'Avola and Syrah blend from Sicilian estate vineyards: the pepper-spice of Syrah amplifies Nero d'Avola's dark fruit and the barrique treatment adds structure and warmth.

Cusumano Jale

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

CusumanoSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Cusumano's premium Chardonnay, Jale, shows the full-sun ripeness of Sicilian Chardonnay through barrique treatment: rich, creamy and tropical with hazelnut-vanilla depth.

Cuvée page →

Cusumano Sagana

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

CusumanoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Sagana is Cusumano's prestige single-vineyard Nero d'Avola from Riesi in central Sicily: opulent, concentrated and barrique-treated to produce a richly chocolate-dark wine of considerable structure.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Tancredi

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Tancredi blends the warmth of Nero d'Avola with Cabernet Sauvignon's structural backbone, producing a Sicilian-Bordeaux hybrid with cedar-cedar depth and concentrated fruit aged gracefully in French barriques.

Cuvée page →

Duca di Salaparuta Duca Enrico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Duca di Salaparuta (Corvo)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Duca Enrico was Sicily's first landmark 100% Nero d'Avola: barrique-aged, structured and concentrated, it defined the modern Sicilian red wine archetype and remains a pillar of the island's wine history.

Cuvée page →

Feudi del Pisciotto Versace Nero d'Avola

★★★★★★★★★★

Feudi del PisciottoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

The Versace-labelled Nero d'Avola from Feudi del Pisciotto is part of the estate's designer fashion-house label series; the wine is a full, smooth and polished Nero d'Avola of immediate approachability.

Feudo Montoni Lagnusa

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Bold

Feudo MontoniSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

The more approachable Feudo Montoni Nero d'Avola; less austere than Vrucara, with a cherry-herb character and the estate's distinctive high-altitude mineral warmth.

Cuvée page →

Feudo Montoni Vrucara

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Feudo MontoniSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Vrucara is from Feudo Montoni's oldest pre-phylloxera Nero d'Avola vines in the high plateau of central Sicily: austere, iron-mineral, demanding years in bottle to reveal its full complexity.

Cuvée page →

Firriato Camelot

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

FirriatoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Firriato's Bordeaux-style blend from the Trapani hinterland: Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot grown in Sicily's sun produce a richly ripe interpretation of the classic Bordeaux blend with a warm cedar-tobacco finish.

Cuvée page →

Girolamo Russo 'A Rina

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Girolamo RussoSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

The prestige Russo single-vineyard: 'A Rina (dialect for 'la rena' - the sand) from some of the deepest sandy volcanic soils on the north slope, producing the most austere and age-demanding wine in the portfolio.

Cuvée page →

Graci Barbabecchi

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

GraciSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

From the Barbabecchi contrada at higher elevation than Arcuria, this Graci red is the most structured and demanding of the range: dense, chalky, and extraordinarily long-lived.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Savoury

GulfiSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

The Baronj contrada produces a more herbal-mineral Nero d'Avola than Bufaleffj, with a finer-grained tannic structure and exceptional length.

Gulfi Nerobufaleffj

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

GulfiSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

The Bufaleffj contrada produces Gulfi's most powerful Nero d'Avola: dense, iron-mineral, dark-fruited and structured with high tannins needing several years to integrate.

Gulfi Nerojbleo

★★★★★★★★★★

GulfiSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Gulfi's entry-level single-contrada Nero d'Avola from the Ibleo plateau: concentrated dark fruit, carob and licorice with firm tannin and a warm volcanic-clay mineral finish.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Rustic

Maggio ViniSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Maggio Vini's Nero d'Avola from the ultra-sun-drenched Maccari-Noto area offers one of the island's richest and warmest expressions of the variety: full-bodied, carob-dark-fruited with licorice depth.

Mandrarossa Timperosse

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Settesoli MandrarossaSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Mandrarossa's Timperosse is one of the rare 100% Petit Verdot bottlings outside Bordeaux: in Sicily's heat the variety reaches complete ripeness, producing an inky, dense and powerfully structured wine with extraordinary colour and longevity.

Cuvée page →

Morgante Don Antonio

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

MorganteSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Don Antonio is Morgante's single-vineyard selection: the richest, most concentrated Nero d'Avola from the estate with barrique aging producing a wine of chocolate-dark-fruit depth and silky structure.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti Siccagno

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Siccagno is the Sicilian dry-farming term for unirrigated cultivation; Occhipinti's varietal Nero d'Avola is richly dark-fruited with a characteristic iron-mineral finish from Vittoria's ferrous soils.

Passopisciaro Contrada R (Rampante)

★★★★★★★★★★

Passopisciaro (Vini Franchetti)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Contrada R Rampante, from the highest-altitude Passopisciaro site at 1,000 metres, produces the most structured and slow-evolving wine of the range: taut, mineral and built for exceptional longevity.

Cuvée page →

Passopisciaro Franchetti

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Passopisciaro (Vini Franchetti)Sicily€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Franchetti is the prestige cuvee of the estate: a powerful blend of Petit Verdot and Cesanese d'Affile from Etna's volcanic soils, combining Bordeaux structure with the island's volcanic energy. A wine of exceptional concentration and longevity.

★★★★★★★★★★

PietradolceSicily€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Vigna Barbagalli is Pietradolce's prestige single-vineyard Etna Rosso from ungrafted pre-phylloxera vines at 850 metres: among the most collectible and age-worthy Nerello Mascalese produced on the north slope.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Planeta's barrique-aged Chardonnay from Ulmo at Menfi became one of the wines that announced Sicily's quality transformation in the 1990s: rich, buttery, confident, with Sicilian sunshine layered through Burgundian oak treatment.

Planeta's Noto DOC Nero d'Avola from the Buonivini contrada captures the south-east of Sicily at its most sun-soaked: rich carob and dark cherry with the warm mineral finish typical of the Noto appellation's limestone soils.

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Santa Cecilia is Planeta's prestige single-estate Nero d'Avola from Noto: rich, structured and mineral with chocolate-dark-fruit depth and the long finish that defines this appellation.

Rapitala Grand Cru is the estate's flagship Chardonnay: a full barrique-fermented Sicilian Chardonnay from the Camporeale hillside estate combining tropical fruit richness with a toasty-creamy Burgundian framework.

Hugonis, named for the founding de la Gatinais family, blends Cabernet Sauvignon's structure with Nero d'Avola's Sicilian warmth in a wine that reflects the estate's Franco-Sicilian origins.

Tasca's Chardonnay from the high Regaleali plateau shows what Sicilian sun and altitude do to Chardonnay: richer and more tropical than Burgundy but retaining the Regaleali site's mineral backbone.

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Tenuta Guado al TassoTuscany€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Pure Cabernet Franc Bolgheri Superiore from Antinori, the estate's single-varietal showcase from a 4-hectare parcel.

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Savoury

Michele SattaTuscany€€€€€€€€Still Red

Michele Satta's Syrah-led blend with Teroldego, the rare Trentino red joining a Bolgheri portfolio.

Cuvée page →

Ornellaia

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Tenuta dell'OrnellaiaTuscany€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Lodovico Antinori's Bolgheri Bordeaux-blend, founded 1981. Bolgheri Superiore DOC under Frescobaldi ownership; Wine Spectator Wine of the Year 2001 for the 1998 vintage.

PolizianoTuscany€€€€€€€€Still Red

Poliziano's Bordeaux-blend Super Tuscan, Cabernet-led IGT from Montepulciano vineyards.

Rocca di FrassinelloTuscany€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Rocca di Frassinello's flagship pure Merlot Maremma Toscana DOC, named for the estate's mascot wild boar.

San FeliceTuscany€€€€€€€€Still Red

The flagship 100 percent Pugnitello bottling, deep and peppery from a grape San Felice rescued from extinction in the 1980s.

San Felice Vigorello

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

San FeliceTuscany€€€€€€€€Still Red

One of the original Super Tuscans, Bordeaux-blended with a slice of the indigenous Pugnitello variety.

Sassicaia

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Tenuta San GuidoTuscany€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The founding Super Tuscan: a Bordeaux blend planted at Bolgheri in 1944 by Mario Incisa della Rocchetta. Granted its own single-estate DOC in 1994.

Cuvée page →

Selvapiana Fornace

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Fattoria SelvapianaTuscany€€€€€€€€Still Red

Bordeaux-blend Super Tuscan from Selvapiana, Cabernet-led Toscana IGT from Rufina vineyards.

Siepi

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Marchesi Mazzei Castello di FonterutoliTuscany€€€€€€€€€Still Red

A 50/50 Sangiovese-Merlot Super Tuscan from the historic Siepi vineyard at 850 feet. Black plum, sandalwood and tobacco over a firm cedar-and-cherry core.

Cuvée page →

Solaia

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Marchesi AntinoriTuscany€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Cabernet-led sibling of Tignanello, denser and more international in profile, with cassis and graphite intensity over a long Tuscan acid spine.

Cuvée page →

Tua Rita Giusto di Notri

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Tua RitaTuscany€€€€€€€€Still Red

Tua Rita's Bordeaux-blend Super Tuscan from Suvereto, the Cabernet-led complement to Redigaffi.

Cuvée page →

Tua Rita Redigaffi

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Tua RitaTuscany€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Tua Rita's flagship 100 percent Merlot Toscana IGT from Suvereto on the Val di Cornia, awarded 100 points by Wine Advocate for the 2000 vintage.

Cuvée page →

Allegrini Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Bold

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Allegrini's estate Amarone Classico blends Corvina and Corvinone in nearly equal measure with a small percentage of Oseleta; the result is a polished, internationally styled Amarone with plush dried-fruit concentration and a refined tannin frame.

Cuvée page →

Allegrini La Poja

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€€€€Still Red

A 100% Corvina IGT aged in French barriques rather than dried: La Poja demonstrates the varietal's structure and ageing potential when treated outside the appassimento framework, producing a powerful, savoury red that rivals Amarone in complexity.

Cuvée page →

Brigaldara Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Bold

BrigaldaraVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Brigaldara's Classico Amarone is known for its precise, traditional approach: careful appassimento in the estate's fruttai and mixed Slavonian/French oak ageing producing a structured, age-worthy expression.

Cuvée page →

Brigaldara Il Vegro Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

BrigaldaraVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Il Vegro is Brigaldara's prestige single-vineyard Amarone from the Il Vegro parcel; higher Oseleta concentration and extended ageing in a mix of small and large casks produce a richer, spicier expression than the estate Classico, with excellent concentration and structure.

Cuvée page →

Cantina Valpolicella Negrar Domaso Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Cantina Valpolicella NegrarVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Domaso is the cooperative's flagship single-vineyard Amarone from the 14-hectare Maternigo hill parcel, showing appreciably greater concentration and mineral precision than the estate-level wine; the earthy, tobacco-cedar style reflects the Maternigo terroir's calcareous character.

Cuvée page →

Cantine Campagnola Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Cantine CampagnolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Campagnola's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico is produced from appassimento-dried Corvina, Corvinone and Rondinella grapes from the Classico hills. Two to three years in large Slavonian oak develop the characteristic dried fruit, dark chocolate and tobacco complexity of the DOCG's flagship wine.

Cuvée page →

Dal Forno Romano Amarone della Valpolicella

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Dal Forno RomanoVeneto€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The most powerful and extracted Amarone on the market: tiny yields, four-month drying, four years in small French oak. Immense concentration of dried fruit, dark chocolate and iron. Built for multi-decade cellaring.

Cuvée page →

Dal Forno Romano Valpolicella Superiore

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Dal Forno RomanoVeneto€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Dal Forno's entry into the Valpolicella range already shows the estate's signature intensity: deeply coloured, barrique-aged and concentrated beyond what the DOC classification implies.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Quintarelli Alzero

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Giuseppe QuintarelliVeneto€€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Quintarelli's Bordeaux varieties treated to the Valpolicella appassimento method: dried Cabernet and Merlot producing extraordinary density, graphite-inflected fruit and a seamlessly long finish.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Quintarelli Primofiore

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Contemplative

Giuseppe QuintarelliVeneto€€€€€€€€€Still Red

A blend of local Corvina with international Cabernet in the Quintarelli style: concentrated, savoury and structured, with the Bordeaux varieties adding colour and spine to the Valpolicella base.

Cuvée page →

Guerrieri Rizzardi Villa Rizzardi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Guerrieri RizzardiVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Guerrieri Rizzardi's Amarone expresses the Classico zone from an estate with over 350 years of Valpolicella history. The extended appassimento (drying) of Corvina and Corvinone grapes concentrates flavour into a wine of great intensity. Three years in Slavonian botti soften the tannins while preserving the dried-fruit character.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Raboso Piave DOC

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Raboso Piave is one of Italy's most ancient and austere red grapes, native to the Piave river plains in Treviso province. Italo Cescon has championed its rehabilitation since the 1960s. High tannin and acid give the wine its characteristic austerity, but serious age-worthiness.

Cuvée page →

Le Salette Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Le SaletteVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Le Salette's estate Amarone Classico, from the high-altitude Fumane vineyards farmed by Monica Scamperle, shows the distinctive freshness and orange-peel note that characterise the estate's high-elevation fruit; a more approachable entry to the Le Salette style than the Pergole Vece.

Cuvée page →

Maculan Fratta Breganze Rosso

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

MaculanVeneto€€€€€€€€Still Red

Fratta is Maculan's Bordeaux-inspired flagship red, one of northeastern Italy's landmark wines. A Cabernet-Merlot blend aged 18 months in new French oak, it rivals top Veneto reds in complexity and ageing potential. Fausto Maculan was among the first producers in the Veneto to vinify Bordeaux varieties at serious quality levels.

Cuvée page →

Masi Campolongo di Torbe Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

From the single Torbe hillside vineyard in the Negrar valley, Campolongo di Torbe is characterised by spicy, tobacco-inflected dried fruit with earthier notes than Masi's more opulent Mazzano bottling.

Cuvée page →

Masi Costasera Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Masi's entry-level Classico Amarone is the house's most widely distributed wine and among the most accessible classic-zone bottlings, balancing dried-cherry concentration with a restrained herbaceous character and good structure.

Cuvée page →

Masi Mazzano Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Mazzano is Masi's most powerful and concentrated single-vineyard Amarone, from the high-altitude Mazzano hill in the S. Ambrogio zone. More opulent and riper than Campolongo, with a chocolatey, rich palate and remarkable ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Masi Toar Rosso Veronese IGT

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still Red

Masi's Toar is a 30% Oseleta-dominant Veronese IGT that deliberately showcases the rarely-used indigenous variety; partial drying of the Oseleta component adds concentration and the grape's characteristic dark spice and iron mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Nicolis Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Nicolis Angelo e FigliVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The Nicolis family's Amarone from the Villa Girardi estate in San Pietro in Cariano reflects a traditional, large-cask aged style with tobacco-leather character and solid structure; consistent quality across multiple vintages.

Cuvée page →

Nicolis Ambrosan Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Nicolis Angelo e FigliVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Ambrosan is the single-parcel selection from Nicolis's oldest vines on the Villa Girardi estate, given an extra year in large Slavonian casks compared to the estate Amarone; a more concentrated and austere expression of tobacco-cedar style with excellent ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta Sant'Antonio Amarone della Valpolicella Campo dei Gigli

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Tenuta Sant'AntonioVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The 20% Oseleta in Campo dei Gigli adds a peppery, dark-spice dimension to the Corvina-Corvinone dried fruit base; from the Mezzane valley outside the Classico zone, the wine shows an earthier, more structured style than comparable Classico Amarone.

Cuvée page →

Tommasi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Tommasi ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Tommasi's estate Amarone Classico represents the fourth-generation family's traditional approach: a high proportion of Corvinone adds floral lift to the dried-cherry concentration while three years in large casks develops structure.

Cuvée page →

Tommasi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Ca' Florian

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Tommasi ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Ca' Florian is Tommasi's single-vineyard flagship Amarone from the old Ca' Florian hillside in the Classico zone, showing dense, chocolatey concentration with elegant tannin structure and long mineral finish.

Cuvée page →

Zenato Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

ZenatoVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Zenato's Classico Amarone incorporates 20% Oseleta adding firm pepper tannin and dark fruit spice to the Corvina-led blend; aged in a combination of large Slavonian and small French oak that bridges traditional and modern styles.

Cuvée page →

Zyme Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Kairos

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

ZymeVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Zyme's Kairos is a modern interpretation of Classico Amarone; former Quintarelli cellarmaster Celestino Gaspari applies small French oak ageing to produce a polished, concentrated expression that bridges traditional intensity with contemporary oak integration.

Cuvée page →

Zyme Harlequin Veronese IGT

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Funky

ZymeVeneto€€€€€€€€€Still Red

A 15-variety blend in the Veronese IGT classification, Harlequin is Gaspari's conceptual statement: every grape variety grown across Zyme's vineyards co-fermented after partial drying, producing a complex, layered wine with extraordinary aromatic diversity.

Cuvée page →

F.X. Pichler Grüner Veltliner Smaragd Frauenweingärten

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Bold · Elegant

Weingut F.X. PichlerWachau€€€€€€€€Still White

Richer, more luxuriant Smaragd Grüner Veltliner from the Frauenweingärten (women's vineyards) Ried: concentrated peach and pepper spice with full body and mineral depth.

Cuvée page →

Holzapfel Prandtauerhof Grüner Veltliner Smaragd Achleiten

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Bold · Savoury

Weingut Holzapfel PrandtauerhofWachau€€€€€€€€Still White

Full-bodied Achleiten Grüner Veltliner Smaragd from Holzapfel: the site's distinctive amphibolite mineral character delivers exotic spice and concentrated stone fruit with full body.
